email_summary: Strauss Group placed Q1 (22 tons) and Q2 (20 tons) orders of Grade A milk powder with Galil Dairy, both delivered on schedule across four shipments. Galil's account manager Yael Shapira proposed a price increase to ₪15.50/kg from Q2 onward, citing rising input costs, but Strauss has not accepted this and both Q2 shipments were invoiced at the contracted ₪14.20/kg. The pricing dispute remains unresolved as of June 2026, with Galil pressing for agreement before Q3 invoicing begins and Strauss yet to formally respond.

contract_terms.payment_terms: Net 45 from invoice date; 2% early payment discount if paid within 10 days
contract_terms.key_penalty: Late delivery beyond 5 business days incurs 1.5% of shipment value per day, capped at 15% of total shipment value.

open_issues.description: Galil proposed ₪15.50/kg from Q2 onward (an 9.2% increase over the contracted ₪14.20/kg), but no written amendment has been signed. Under contract Section 4.2, the base price is fixed for the 24-month term and any change requires mutual written amendment; Galil's unilateral email notice does not satisfy this requirement.
open_issues.source_ref: Email 2026-03-03 from Yael Shapira; contract STR-DAIRY-2025-014, Section 4.2 (Price Cap)

open_issues.description: Yael Shapira has followed up three times (March 3, May 5, June 3) on the pricing dispute and has not received a substantive resolution or committed timeline from Strauss; a call was proposed for mid-May but no outcome is documented.
Galil's June 3 email requested confirmation of 20 tons for Q3 (July–August) but Strauss has not replied; Q3 planning is stalled pending resolution of the pricing dispute.
open_issues.source_ref: Email 2026-06-03 from Yael Shapira, subject: Q2 pricing + Q3 planning

commodity_benchmark: The spot dairy commodity price has declined from approximately ₪17.03 in early April 2026 to ₪16.19 by late June, a roughly 5% drop over the period — significantly above the contracted ₪14.20/kg but trending downward, weakening Galil's market-pressure argument for a price increase.
heads_up: Galil's proposed ₪15.50/kg price increase is a direct violation of Section 4.2 of contract STR-DAIRY-2025-014, which fixes the price at ₪14.20/kg for the full 24-month term without a mutual written amendment — Strauss is under no contractual obligation to accept it, and the declining commodity benchmark (down ~5% since April) further undermines Galil's justification.
negotiation_brief: Strauss holds a strong contractual position: the active contract (STR-DAIRY-2025-014, running to August 2027) explicitly locks the price at ₪14.20/kg under Section 4.2, and Galil's ₪15.50/kg demand has no contractual force without a signed written amendment. Delivery performance has been excellent — all four shipments arrived on schedule — so there is no leverage to apply on service grounds, but equally no concession is owed. The commodity benchmark is running well above the contract price (≈₪16–17/kg vs ₪14.20/kg), meaning Galil does face genuine cost pressure, but the market has been falling for two months, which weakens their urgency argument and gives Strauss room to stall or negotiate a smaller adjustment if a goodwill concession is strategically warranted. Walking in: hold the ₪14.20/kg line for Q3, acknowledge the market context without prejudice, and if any price movement is considered, insist on a formal written amendment with a capped rate and a defined review schedule — do not allow an open-ended escalation.

financial_signals.delivery_reliability_score: Good — all four shipments (Q1 and Q2) dispatched and arrived on schedule, each confirmed within the 5-business-day window.

benchmark.unit_label: USD per hundredweight (CME Class III Milk)
benchmark.source: CME Class III Milk Futures (DC=F) via Yahoo Finance
benchmark.note: CME dairy futures are thinly traded and the series may be incomplete. Treat as directional indicator only, not a precise benchmark for direct negotiation use.

raw_emails.subject: Q1 order confirmation — Galil
raw_emails.body:
Yael,

Shalom. Happy new year. We'd like to confirm our Q1 order of 22 tons Grade A milk powder. Please send a delivery schedule and confirm pricing is still per the contract.

Thanks,
David

raw_emails.subject: RE: Q1 order confirmation — Galil
raw_emails.body:
David,

Shana tova and happy new year! Great to hear from you.

Confirmed — 22 tons Grade A at ₪14.20/kg as per the contract. We'll split into two shipments: first 11 tons around January 20, second 11 tons around February 10. I'll have Oren in logistics send you the schedule.

Also, as a reminder for your finance team — Galil offers a 2.5% discount on invoices settled within 10 days of issue date. Standard terms are net 30. Just flag it to them if they want to take advantage.

Yael

raw_emails.subject: Delivery schedule — Q1 Galil order
raw_emails.body:
Hi David,

This is Oren from logistics. Confirming Q1 schedule:
- Shipment 1: 11 tons, departure January 19, ETA your site January 22
- Shipment 2: 11 tons, departure February 9, ETA February 12

Please send unloading appointment details for each.

Oren

raw_emails.subject: Shipment 1 dispatched
raw_emails.body:
David,

Shipment 1 (11 tons) dispatched this morning, truck IL-3847. ETA Holon plant January 22 as scheduled. As with our previous deliveries, this shipment departed within the 5 business days from order confirmation that we quoted.

Oren

raw_emails.subject: Shipment 2 dispatched
raw_emails.body:
David,

Shipment 2 (11 tons) dispatched, truck IL-4102. ETA February 12. Q1 order complete after this delivery — again within the 5-business-day window, same as Shipment 1.

Oren

raw_emails.subject: Q2 order — Galil
raw_emails.body:
Yael,

Looking ahead to Q2 — we'll need approximately 20 tons Grade A again, split into two deliveries in April and May. Can you confirm availability and pricing?

David

raw_emails.subject: RE: Q2 order — Galil + market update
raw_emails.body:
David,

Q2 — yes, we have availability for 20 tons. Happy to supply.

One thing I want to mention while I have you — the milk powder market has moved quite a bit since we signed the contract last September. Our input costs have gone up significantly and we're reviewing pricing across customers for Q2. Given where the market is right now, we'll need to move to ₪15.50/kg from Q2 onward. I know it's a step up but the economics have really changed.

Let me know your thoughts on the Q2 order and the pricing.

Yael

raw_emails.subject: RE: Q2 order — Galil
raw_emails.body:
Yael,

Noted on the pricing — I'll need to review this with my team internally. On the Q2 order itself, can you go ahead and send a delivery proposal for the 20 tons in April and May? We can sort out the commercial discussion separately.

David

raw_emails.subject: Q2 delivery proposal
raw_emails.body:
Hi David,

Proposed Q2 schedule:
- Shipment 1: 10 tons, week of April 13
- Shipment 2: 10 tons, week of May 11

Please confirm and we'll issue the pro-forma.

Oren

raw_emails.subject: RE: Q2 delivery proposal — confirmed
raw_emails.body:
Oren,

Delivery schedule confirmed. Please go ahead with the pro-forma.

Yael — I'm still reviewing the pricing discussion with my manager. I'll come back to you separately on that.

David

raw_emails.subject: Q2 Shipment 1 dispatched
raw_emails.body:
David,

First Q2 shipment (10 tons) dispatched this morning. ETA April 16 — within the 5-business-day window as always.

Oren

raw_emails.subject: Pricing — following up
raw_emails.body:
David,

I want to follow up on the Q2 pricing. I raised ₪15.50/kg in early March and haven't heard back. The invoice for Shipment 1 still shows ₪14.20/kg. We need to get alignment on this before Shipment 2 goes out.

Also just a reminder — the 2.5% early payment discount is still available on the Shipment 1 invoice if your finance team processes within 10 days of issue date. It expires soon.

Can we speak this week?

Yael

raw_emails.subject: RE: Pricing
raw_emails.body:
Yael,

Appreciate you following up. This is still being reviewed on our end. I cannot commit to a price change via email — this would need to go through a formal process including a written amendment. Let's schedule a call next week.

David

raw_emails.subject: Q2 Shipment 2 dispatched
raw_emails.body:
David,

Second Q2 shipment (10 tons) dispatched today, truck IL-4883. ETA May 14. Q2 complete.

Oren

raw_emails.subject: Q2 pricing + Q3 planning
raw_emails.body:
David,

Following up again on the ₪15.50/kg — we've now had two shipments go out at the old rate and I really need to get this resolved before Q3 invoicing starts. I understand you need to go through a formal process but can you give me a timeline?

Also, for Q3 we are planning similar volumes — can you confirm 20 tons for July-August?

Yael

raw_contracts.supplier_04_galil_dairy.md:
# Contract Summary — Galil Dairy Suppliers

**Contract ID:** STR-DAIRY-2025-014
**Supplier:** Galil Dairy Suppliers (Israel)
**Category:** Dairy — Grade A milk powder
**Effective Date:** 2025-09-01
**Term:** 24 months
**Renewal Date:** 2027-08-31 (auto-renews unless either party gives 90 days notice)

## Payment Terms
Net 45 from invoice date. 2% early payment discount if paid within 10 days.

## Volume Commitment
Minimum 18 tons/quarter, maximum 30 tons/quarter without prior written
agreement. Strauss has first-right-of-refusal on any volume above 30
tons/quarter before Galil offers it to other buyers.

## Pricing
Base price: ₪14.20/kg (approx. $3.85/kg at contract signing) for Grade A
milk powder, fixed for the contract term.

**Section 4.2 (Price Cap):** The base price shall not exceed ₪14.20/kg
during the 24-month contract term, except by mutual written amendment
signed by both parties. Market-driven price adjustments require minimum
30 days written notice and Strauss approval before taking effect.

## Penalty Clauses
- Late delivery beyond 5 business days: 1.5% of shipment value per day,
  capped at 15% of total shipment value.
- Quality failure (below Grade A spec on inspection): full replacement at
  supplier cost, no charge to Strauss.

## Notes
This is the contract that should be cross-referenced against supplier 4's
email thread once generated. Any email mentioning a price above ₪14.20/kg
(~$3.85/kg) without referencing a written amendment is a direct violation
of Section 4.2, this is the planted inconsistency the prototype should
surface.
